topics: 🪵 גזל מריש ובנאו בבירה — Beit Shammai vs. Beit Hillel
🌿 תקנת השבים and יישוב ארץ ישראל — why the policy bends for repenters
🐂 R' Meir on aged slaves: 'הרי שלך לפניך'
🪙 Coin invalidated by malkhut vs. medina
🔨 Artisans and builders who damage — when is the craftsman liable?

overview.title: 📖 Overview — Repentance, Transformation, and the Craftsman
overview.label: The sugya:
overview.text: Daf 38 surveys a series of cases where the gezeilah has changed since the theft. Beit Shammai vs. Beit Hillel famously dispute the stolen beam built into a building — Hillel rules in favor of תקנת שבים. R' Yaakov bar Acha grounds Hillel's position in יישוב ארץ ישראל. The amoraim then carve up the rules for aged animals and slaves, spoiled fruit, invalidated coins, leavened matter through Pesach, and artisans who break what they were hired to repair.
overview.hebrewSource: גָּזַל מָרִישׁ וּבְנָאוֹ בַבִּירָה. בֵּית שַׁמַּי אוֹמְרִים. יְקַעְקֵעַ כָּל הַבִּירָה כוּלָּהּ וְיִתְּנֶינּוּ לוֹ. וּבֵית הִלֵּל אוֹמְרִים. יִתֵּן לוֹ דָמָיו מִפְּנֵי תַקָּנַת שָׁבִים

slides.title: 🏛️ The Stolen Beam Built into a Building
slides.subtitle: Beit Shammai and Beit Hillel split on what the penitent must restore.

lines.speaker: BS
lines.text: Beit Shammai: יְקַעְקֵעַ כָּל הַבִּירָה כוּלָּהּ וְיִתְּנֶינּוּ לוֹ — he must tear down the whole building and return the actual beam.

lines.speaker: BH
lines.text: Beit Hillel: יִתֵּן לוֹ דָמָיו מִפְּנֵי תַקָּנַת שָׁבִים — he just pays its value, to support the penitents.

lines.speaker: RYbA
lines.text: R' Yaakov bar Acha in the name of R' Yehoshua ben Levi: the reason is also יִישׁוּב אֶרֶץ יִשְׂרָאֵל — settlement of the Land.

lines.speaker: RNbY
lines.text: Rav Nachman bar Yitzchak: would we really refuse a man who put his bundle in another's ruin temporarily? Once he heard the ארץ ישראל reason — that's the answer.

callout.label: Why this matters:
callout.text: The halakhah is not pure restitution. It is calibrated by social policy: encourage teshuva, encourage building the land.

slides.title: 🌿 Building on Someone Else's Ruin
slides.subtitle: When can a builder recover his investment?

columns.label: Without permission
columns.content:
שָׁמִין לוֹ וְיָדוֹ עַל הַתַּחְתּוֹנָה
Lower hand

columns.label: With permission
columns.content:
שָׁמִין לוֹ וְיָדוֹ עַל הָעֶלְיוֹנָה
Upper hand

nodes.content: Rav: even at unfit places, we estimate but employ מַעֲרִימִין — letting him stay in summer and winter
All policy threads return to: יִישׁוּב אֶרֶץ יִשְׂרָאֵל

callout.label: Key insight:
callout.text: The 'lower/upper hand' formula is the Yerushalmi's standard mechanism for permission-based fair valuation.

slides.title: 🐂 When Does 'הרי שלך לפניך' Apply?
slides.subtitle: R' Meir vs. the Tanna Kamma — and the amoraic refinements.

boxes.title: 📜 Mishnah
items: R' Meir: for slaves only — 'הרי שלך לפניך'

boxes.title: 🐄 Rav Huna
items: Stole bull → it aged = 'הרי שלך לפניך'
Drawing the line at species-level change

boxes.title: 🐂 Shmuel
items: Even calf → bull, says 'הרי שלך לפניך'

boxes.title: ✂️ Rav Karna
items: Pays ד' or ה' of the calf's value

callout.label: R' Zeira & Rav Chisda:
callout.text: Stole fat → became thin: must replace the fat. Stole thin → fattened: returns it as is. The gazlan never benefits, never overpays.

slides.title: 🪙 What Counts as 'Returnable as Is'?
slides.subtitle: Mishnah 9:3 — when the object is technically intact but its value has collapsed.

callout.label: Rav Huna vs. Shmuel:
callout.text: Rav Huna: only if government invalidated the coin. If a province did, he says 'הרי שלך לפניך'. Shmuel: even if government invalidated — 'הרי שלך לפניך'.

slides.title: 🔨 Artisans, Builders, and the Last Nail
slides.subtitle: Mishnah 9:4 — when does the craftsman become the responsible party?

steps.content: 1️⃣ Owner gives item to artisans to repair and they ruined it → liable to pay.
2️⃣ Carpenter receives chest/box/cupboard and breaks it → liable.
3️⃣ Builder hired to take down a wall and damages stones → liable.
4️⃣ Wall collapsed on the other side → exempt. But if from the pounding (מַחֲמַת הַמַּכָּה) → liable.
5️⃣ Rav Huna: only if he drove in the last nail — קָבַע בּוֹ מַסְמֵר הָאַחֲרוֹן כְּדֵי לְזַכּוֹת לְכֵלָיו.

callout.label: The principle:
callout.text: Liability transfers when the artisan acquires craftsman's rights in the object — symbolized by the final nail. Until then, his actions are framed as the owner's loss.

summary.principle: When the gezeilah has changed, the law calibrates between strict restitution and social policies — תקנת שבים, יישוב ארץ ישראל, and protecting the integrity of artisans' work.

flowSteps.content: Beit Hillel: pays דמיו מפני תקנת שבים

🎓 Key Principle:
Restitution law is shaped not just by ownership but by policy — making space for repentance, settlement, and the working professions.

quiz.q: Why does Beit Hillel rule that the robber of the beam pays its value rather than tearing down the building?
o: Because the building is too valuable
מִפְּנֵי תַקָּנַת שָׁבִים — to support the penitents
Because Beit Shammai is in the minority
Because the original owner waived his rights
quiz.e: Beit Hillel says: 'יתן לו דמיו מפני תקנת שבים' — to encourage repentance, the law accepts payment instead of demolition.

quiz.q: What additional reason does R' Yaakov bar Acha give in the name of R' Yehoshua ben Levi?
o: פְּגַם מִשְׁפָּחָה
יִישׁוּב אֶרֶץ יִשְׂרָאֵל
תִּיקּוּן הָעוֹלָם
קְנַס
quiz.e: R' Yaakov bar Acha grounds the lenient ruling in the policy of settling the Land of Israel.

quiz.q: According to R' Meir, what does the gazlan say about aged slaves?
o: He pays double
הֲרֵי שֶׁלָּךְ לְפָנֶיךָ — here is your property as is
He pays the value at the time of the robbery
He must replace them with younger slaves
quiz.e: Mishnah: 'רבי מאיר אומר יאמר לו בעבדים הרי שלך לפניך.' Rav Yehudah ruled: halakhah follows R' Meir.

quiz.q: How do Rav Huna and Shmuel disagree about an invalidated coin?
o: They agree completely
Rav Huna: only government invalidation = pays. Shmuel: even government invalidation → 'הרי שלך לפניך'
Rav Huna: he must replace it with gold; Shmuel: with silver
Both say it must be returned in pieces
quiz.e: Rav Huna distinguishes malkhut from medina; Shmuel says even malkhut-invalidation lets the gazlan say 'הרי שלך לפניך'.

quiz.q: According to Rav Huna, when does an artisan become liable for damaging the item?
o: Only after delivering the work
Only when he drove in the last nail (מַסְמֵר הָאַחֲרוֹן) — acquiring his utensils' rights
Once payment is given
Never — only the owner is liable
quiz.e: Rav Huna: 'והוא שקבע בו מסמר האחרון כדי לזכות לכליו.' Liability arrives with the artisan's acquisition of work-rights.
